config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dasar seseorang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati terlebih dahulu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a blanket rule. From a U.S. perspective, we have plenty of history showing that “the common good” can be used to excuse discrimination, censorship, and abuse of power, especially against minorities. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute when they directly harm others, so there has to be a narrow balance rather than automatic deference to society. In practice, I would rather err on the side of protecting individual liberty and forcing society to justify any limit very carefully.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau melindungi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Memang benar hak individu tidak boleh dipakai untuk merugikan orang lain, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, sempit, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan dalih otomatis. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru lahir ketika negara dan mayoritas disiplin menghormati hak dasar tiap orang, bukan ketika individu diminta terus mengalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. The strongest point from the other side is that individual rights are not a license to harm other people, and I accept that limits are sometimes necessary. But those limits should be narrow, specific, and heavily scrutinized, not treated as a default rule that society always wins. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first and then proves, case by case, why any restriction is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak klaim bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari konteks Indonesia, saya justru melihat frasa “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau melanggengkan kuasa. Hak individu memang tidak absolut kalau merugikan orang lain, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, spesifik, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan alasan otomatis. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun bukan dengan meminta individu terus mengalah, melainkan dengan memastikan mayoritas dan negara patuh pada batas hak dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. In the United States, I have seen how language about the "common good" can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, so I am very wary of giving society the default advantage. That said, I do not believe individual rights are absolute when someone is clearly harming other people, but those limits should be narrow, specific, and justified case by case. A healthy society protects rights first and only restricts them when there is a real and provable necessity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
